recalls.Description: Astemrey has announced a recall of its Y'all Can DIY bioethanol fireplace fuel containers due to a serious risk of flash fire. These containers are clear with a black top and feature a label that includes the brand name and product information in white and orange, along with safety claims such as 'No Soot' and 'Clean Burning.' However, they do not meet the required safety standards for portable fuel containers, posing a significant danger to consumers.
recalls.Title: Astemrey Issues Recall for Y'all Can DIY Bioethanol Fireplace Fuel Containers Due to Flash Fire Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Astemrey via email at contact@astemrey.com.

Products.Name: Y'all Can DIY Bioethanol Fireplace Fuel Containers

Images.Caption: Recalled Y'all Can DIY Liquid Bioethanol 1000ml fuel container
Recalled Y'all Can DIY Liquid Bioethanol 1000ml fuel full product label

Manufacturers.Name: STARKİM KİMYA SAN.TİC. A.Ş, of Turkey

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from April 2025 to July 2025, priced around $19 for single packs and $31 for three-packs.

Distributors.Name: STARKIM KIMYA SAN.TIC. A.S, of Turkey

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire due to lack of required flame mitigation devices.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product, label it 'DO NOT USE,' and contact Astemrey for a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Astemrey recalls Y'all Can DIY bioethanol fireplace fuel due to flash fire risks. Contact for free replacement and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: Scepter B62 gas and oil fuel containers are being recalled due to serious safety hazards. These containers are red with black caps and yellow spouts, featuring dual chambers with a 6-liter gas capacity and a 2.5-liter oil capacity. They are marked with "Scepter" and "Gasoline Danger" on the side, along with a Fire Marshall stamp near the fuel spout. The design fails to meet mandatory safety standards, increasing the risk of flash fires and child poisoning. The spouts are not child-resistant, posing additional dangers.
recalls.Title: Scepter Gas and Oil Fuel Containers Recalled Due to Fire and Poisoning Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Canada Prep via email at info@canadaprepandship.com or visit https://canadaprepandship.com/recalls. Click on the "Recall" section at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Scepter Gas and Oil Fuel Containers

Images.Caption: Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – front
Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – side

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from December 2024 to August 2025, priced around $36.

Importers.Name: Lowe Opco Inc., dba, Canada Prep and Ship, of Canada

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire and child poisoning due to non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the containers, keep them away from children, and contact Canada Prep for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Scepter fuel containers recalled due to fire and poisoning risks. Contact Canada Prep for a refund and safety information.

recalls.Description:
Jonas C is recalling their Long-Haul 5L ATV gas cans, which are identified by the labels "LONG-HAUL," "TZYC," and "PATENT PENDING." These black fuel containers come with multiple spout components and bear various warnings related to gasoline use. The product fails to meet federal child-resistant closure requirements, posing significant risks of burns, fires, and poisoning to children, potentially leading to severe injuries or fatalities.

Consumers are urged to stop using these gas cans immediately and ensure they are kept out of children's reach. For those who purchased these containers, Jonas C is offering a full refund for items bought after January 1, 2024, and a partial refund for those purchased before December 31, 2023. The company, along with Amazon, is reaching out to all known buyers to facilitate the return process.
recalls.Title: Jonas C Recalls Long-Haul 5L ATV Gas Cans Due to Serious Burn and Poisoning Risks; Non-Compliant with Child Safety Regulations

Images.Caption: Recalled Long-Haul Portable Fuel Container with Accessories

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from January 2023 to October 2024, priced around $50.

Distributors.Name: Cheng du shi ri teng mao yi you xian gong si, DBA Jonas C, of London, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Risk of burns, fires, and poisoning due to non-child-resistant closures

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gas cans, keep them away from children, and contact Jonas C for return or disposal instructions to receive a refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Jonas C recalls Long-Haul 5L ATV gas cans due to serious burn and poisoning hazards. Immediate action required for refunds.

recalls.Description: Worthington Enterprises has issued a recall for EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinders identified by model number EVAS - M0859. These cylinders have serial numbers starting with 'W' and date codes of either '06-23' or '07-23', which can be found stamped on the collar. Initially sold under the AmeriGas label, these cylinders may have been exchanged at various propane distributors that applied their own branding.
recalls.Title: Recall of EVAS 20 lb. Propane Exchange Cylinders by Worthington Enterprises Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Worthington at 888-520-1304 from Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, email at propanecylinders@realtimeresults.net, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/PropaneCylinders.

Images.Caption: Location of model number of recalled EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinder
Scan QR Code for propane exchange retail locations

Retailers.Name: AmeriGas propane cylinder exchange locations in New Hampshire and the Boston Metropolitan area from July to August 2023, or from other propane cylinder exchange suppliers thereafter, priced between $20 and $80.

Distributors.Name: AmeriGas, of King of Prussia, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Potential gas leakage creating a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled cylinders immediately, close the top valve, and return them to any AmeriGas Propane Exchange location for a free replacement. For locations, visit https://www.amerigas.com/locations/find-propane. Ensure the valve is fully closed when returning.

recalls.metaDescription: Worthington recalls EVAS 20 lb. propane exchange cylinders due to fire hazards. Learn how to return and replace affected cylinders.
